metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4180
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3A-D: The mEJC amplitudes in both dor35 and dor36 mutants (orange) were larger compared with controls and were rescued by restoring the expression of Dor ubiquitously (daGAL4; green).
Figure 3F: The quantal content (EJC/mEJC) in dor mutants is reduced compared with controls suggesting that in dor mutants, fewer vesicles fuse with the synaptic membrane upon nerve stimulation.
Figure S2B: Quantification of synaptic vesicle diameter using transmission EM (TEM) of dor35 and dor36 mutant synaptic boutons does not reveal a difference in mean synaptic vesicle size compared with controls indicating that the effect on mEJCs is not due to larger vesicles that harbour more neurotransmitters.

To test whether fewer vesicles fuse upon stimulation in dor mutants, FM 1-43 dye labelling of NMJ nerve terminal was done to trace new vesicles that are trafficked from plasma membrane.
Figure 3G-H: dor mutants internalize less dye and the effect was rescued by restoring the expression of Dor ubiquitously (GAL4 ; green) or presynaptically (vGlutGAL4; purple).
Figure S2B-D: Synaptic vesicle number, the presence of endocytic intermediates, and the number of vesicles tethered at presynaptic release sites were not different in dor mutants.
Figure 3I-J: RRP is ~30% smaller in dor mutants compared with controls suggesting the defect in quantal content observed in dor mutants may be attributed, at least in part, to a smaller RRP.

The paper demonstrates that dor (VPS18) plays a role in endosomal trafficking of synaptic vesicles. Due to a lack of such ontology term, the parent term "regulation of synaptic vesicle cycle" was used.. Experimental description: The dor stocks were obtained from the Bloomington Stock Center Indiana (BL) or from Vienna Drosophila RNAi Centre. The dor levels in the mutants were tested via immunoblotting experiment (Figure 1D)
